# ADR 0020: Agent Harness concept anchor + session-intake recipe

## Context

Operator main-goal directive (2026-06-12, chief-architect session on Epic #13012): *"a future session must easily be capable to pick up the concept, and know where we are and what's up next. could be an ADR and or a project board. we do NOT want to read 20 pages each session."*

The harness concept currently lives across: closed Discussion #10119 (36KB archaeology), Epic #13012 (body + maintained plan-of-record comment), sub-epics/leaves, and tonight's decision records. Each surface is correct at its altitude — but a fresh session has no single ≤-few-pages concept anchor, and "where are we" requires thread-walking. The same evening produced two operator token-economics corrections (work items ≠ ideation venues; epic-review cap 2) — the intake surface must honor that principle structurally.

## The Problem

Session-intake cost compounds: every future session (mine, a peer's, a post-compaction recovery) pays the full archaeology unless a durable, small, versioned concept anchor exists. Project boards carry live state but not the WHY/WHAT; epic bodies carry solution shape but evolve; Discussions are archaeology. The missing piece is the stable narrative layer — exactly what ADRs are for (precedent: ADR 0018 identity SSOT, ADR 0019 AiConfig SSOT — both serve as cold-read anchors today).

## The Architectural Reality

- ADRs live in `learn/agentos/decisions/` (next free number: 0020; sibling shapes to lift: `0018-neo-identity-source-of-truth-model.md`, `0019-aiconfig-reactive-provider-ssot.md`).
- The intake stack this ADR completes: **(1) ADR 0020** (concept, stable) → **(2) [Project board 13](https://github.com/orgs/neomjs/projects/13)** (live state: epics/leaves/status) → **(3) the target work item** (detail). Bounded cold-read.
- Content the ADR must anchor (all converged on #13012 as of 2026-06-12): the product bar (compete with Claude Desktop / Claude Code tab); simplicity-first (download → productive); the two entry modes (bring-your-harness via extended-NL MCP; native Anthropic-account login); two-axis architecture (multi-window Neo app + extended-NL coordination substrate); Electron decision + rationale (always Chromium + Node, Agent OS in-process, Tauri retired); hemisphere placement (UI in `apps/` replacing the agentos PoC; fleet services in `ai/`; PATs Brain-side only); three pillars (fleet manager → conversational creation → deploy plane); git-tooling requirement; streaming + buffered chat rendering; the binding guardrails (Topological-Locking-before-multi-writer, benchmark-before-perf-claims, session-id canonicalization `#12984`, restart affordances, breadcrumb scope); FOSS posture (MIT engine + harness; monetize the hosted plane).

## The Fix

One PR: author `learn/agentos/decisions/0020-agent-harness-concept.md` per the sibling ADR conventions (status/context/decision/consequences shape), ≤ a few pages, ending with the **session-intake recipe** (the 3-step stack above) as an explicit section. Wire any docs-tree registration the decisions folder requires (verify against how 0018/0019 are wired). The ADR cites Epic #13012 as the live decomposition root and Discussion #10119 as archaeology-only.

Out of scope: the roadmap/vision public-surface rewrite (operator named it as a possible companion — separate leaf when called; this ADR is the internal anchor), the AGENTS-substrate breadcrumb PR (separate guardrail leaf, may reference the ADR once it exists).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `learn/agentos/decisions/0020-agent-harness-concept.md` exists, sibling-convention-compliant, ≤ a few pages.
- [ ] Carries ALL converged decisions listed under Architectural Reality (spot-checkable against #13012's body at merge time).
- [ ] Ends with the explicit session-intake recipe (ADR → board 13 → target item).
- [ ] Cold-read test: a fresh session reading ONLY the ADR + glancing board 13 can state the product bar, the current pillar, and the next actionable leaves without opening #10119.
- [ ] Decision Record impact: `aligned-with ADR 0018` (identity surfaces), no conflicts.

Release classification: post-release (product line).

## Related
